questions.text: Which of the following statements about light is correct?
options: Light travels in a curved path
Light travels in a straight line
Light travels faster in water than in air
questions.explanation: Light travels in a straight line, a property known as rectilinear propagation.

questions.text: The change in the direction of light by a mirror is called:
questions.explanation: Reflection is the bouncing back of light when it strikes a polished surface like a mirror.

questions.text: An image that can be obtained on a screen is called a:
questions.explanation: Real images are formed by the actual intersection of light rays and can be caught on a screen.

questions.text: An image that cannot be obtained on a screen is called a:
questions.explanation: Virtual images are formed when light rays appear to meet at a point; they cannot be caught on a screen.

questions.text: The image formed by a plane mirror is always:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror forms an upright (erect) image that cannot be caught on a screen (virtual).

questions.text: In a plane mirror, the left side of the object appears on the right side of the image. This is called:
questions.explanation: Lateral inversion is the side-to-side reversal of an object in a mirror.

questions.text: Which of the following is used to see the rear view in vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view and always form an erect, diminished image.

questions.text: A mirror with a reflecting surface curved inwards is a:
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors have a reflecting surface that caves in, like the inside of a spoon.

questions.text: A mirror with a reflecting surface curved outwards is a:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors have a reflecting surface that bulges out, like the back of a spoon.

questions.text: Dentists use which type of mirror to see an enlarged image of teeth?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors form a magnified, virtual, and erect image when the object is very close.

questions.text: The reflectors of torches and headlights of cars are ________ in shape.
questions.explanation: Concave reflectors help in producing a powerful parallel beam of light.

questions.text: Which type of lens is thicker in the middle than at the edges?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses converge light rays and are thicker at the center.

questions.text: Which type of lens is thinner in the middle than at the edges?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses diverge light rays and are thinner at the center.

questions.text: A convex lens is also known as a ________ lens.
questions.explanation: It converges (brings together) parallel rays of light falling on it.

questions.text: A concave lens is also known as a ________ lens.
questions.explanation: It diverges (spreads out) light rays falling on it.

questions.text: Which of the following can be used as a magnifying glass?
questions.explanation: A convex lens forms a magnified, virtual, and erect image of an object placed close to it.

questions.text: White light is composed of ________ colors.
questions.explanation: Sunlight (white light) consists of seven colors: VIBGYOR.

questions.text: The splitting of white light into its constituent colors is called:
questions.explanation: Dispersion occurs when white light passes through a prism, forming a spectrum.

questions.text: Newton's disc appears ________ in color when rotated quickly.
questions.explanation: The mixing of the seven colors on the rotating disc gives the perception of white color.

questions.text: A rainbow is always formed in a direction ________ to that of the sun.
questions.explanation: Rainbows appear when sunlight is reflected and dispersed by raindrops in the opposite direction of the sun.

questions.text: The distance of the image from a plane mirror is:
options: Depends on the size of the mirror
questions.explanation: In a plane mirror, the image is as far behind the mirror as the object is in front of it.

questions.text: A concave lens always forms an image which is:
questions.explanation: Concave lenses always produce virtual, upright, and smaller images, regardless of the object's position.

questions.text: Which of these objects does not allow light to pass through it?
questions.explanation: Wood is an opaque object; it blocks light and forms a shadow.

questions.text: A polished or shiny surface can act as a:
questions.explanation: Shiny surfaces reflect light effectively, behaving like a mirror.

questions.text: What type of image is formed on a cinema screen?
questions.explanation: Since the image is projected on a screen, it must be a real image.

questions.text: In a plane mirror, the size of the image is ________ compared to the object.
questions.explanation: A plane mirror does not change the size of the object's image.

questions.text: Which mirror can form both real and virtual images?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors can form real or virtual images depending on how close the object is to the mirror.

questions.text: The path of light is:
questions.explanation: Rectilinear propagation means light travels in straight lines.

questions.text: Compact discs (CDs) show many colors when sunlight falls on them due to:
questions.explanation: The surface of a CD acts as a grating that disperses white light into its colors.

questions.text: Why is 'AMBULANCE' written in a laterally inverted way on vehicles?
options: So drivers in front can read it correctly in their mirrors
questions.explanation: When seen in a rear-view mirror, the lateral inversion of the mirror makes the word appear correctly.

questions.text: A real image is always:
questions.explanation: Real images formed by mirrors and lenses are always upside down (inverted).

questions.text: Which color of light deviates the most when passing through a prism?
questions.explanation: Violet light has the shortest wavelength and bends the most.

questions.text: Which color of light deviates the least when passing through a prism?
questions.explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength and bends the least.

questions.text: A virtual image is always:
questions.explanation: Virtual images are always upright (erect).

questions.text: The middle color in the spectrum of white light is:
questions.explanation: In VIBGYOR, Green is the middle color.

questions.text: Can a convex mirror ever form a real image?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always form virtual images of real objects.

questions.text: Which of these is a non-luminous object?
questions.explanation: The moon does not emit its own light; it reflects sunlight.

questions.text: If you stand 2 meters away from a plane mirror, the distance between you and your image is:
questions.explanation: 2m (object to mirror) + 2m (mirror to image) = 4m.

questions.text: The geometric center of the reflecting surface of a spherical mirror is called:
questions.explanation: The pole (P) is the center point of the spherical mirror surface.

questions.text: Shadows are formed because light:
options: Travels in straight lines and is blocked by opaque objects
questions.explanation: Opaque objects block the straight path of light, creating a dark region called a shadow.
